_doc: Human-centered (心理/共情/道德/价值观/医疗对齐) lineage map.

11292.note: Awad et al., Nature 2018. Online experimental platform collecting 40M decisions from 233 countries on autonomous-vehicle trolley dilemmas. Identified universal vs cultural moral preferences.
critiques_received: MoReBench (2025): outcome-only forced choice; ignores procedural reasoning.
MFT replication (2023): 9-attribute design doesn't map cleanly onto established moral foundations.
Moral stories (2021): forced binary trolley choices oversimplify situated reasoning.
11292.self_acknowledged_limits: Self-selected online participants; trolley-style scenarios highly stylized; cross-cultural framing limited.

11092.note: 12k structured narratives: norm + situation + intention + moral action + immoral action + consequence. Tests whether models understand norm-violating reasoning chains.
critiques_received: MoReBench (2025): outcome labels embed annotator bias; pluralism not modeled.
CMoralEval (2024): English/Western norms only.
Educator-LLM (2025): norms drawn from US Reddit; not transferable to professional contexts.
11092.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag US-centric norms; binary moral/immoral labels may suppress disagreement.

10776.note: Hendrycks et al. 130k+ examples covering Justice, Deontology, Virtue, Utilitarianism, Commonsense moral judgments — five sub-benchmarks. Crowd-annotated.
critiques_received: MoReBench (2025): outcome-only; doesn't test moral reasoning chains.
Scherrer 2023 (MoralChoice): low-ambiguity items only; doesn't probe disagreement.
CMoralEval (2024) / MFQ-2 (2023): five frameworks chosen by authors; some categories thin (e.g., Virtue 2.5k).
Educator-LLM (2025): no professional context; abstracts away role-bound duties.
10776.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag MTurk crowdsourcing variance; English; some sub-benchmarks (Virtue) limited to single-word judgments.

12942.note: 32k r/AmItheAsshole anecdotes with community verdicts (YTA/NTA/ESH/NAH); tests social/moral judgment in everyday situations.
critiques_received: MoReBench (2025): outcome-only; doesn't model procedural reasoning.
CMoralEval (2024): English-only Reddit demographic skew.
VITAL/HumaniBench: lacks healthcare/professional alignment context.
12942.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag Reddit demographic skew (young Western); community verdicts noisy; binary labels suppress moral pluralism.

12918.note: Zakharin & Bates 2023: validation/replication of Moral Foundations Questionnaire 2 (Care/Equality/Proportionality/Loyalty/Authority/Purity). Psychology study, not benchmark — but provides theoretical scaffold for many later HC benchmarks.
critiques_received: Construct validity in LLM evals: MFQ designed for human surveys; transferring to LLMs (e.g., MoralChoice using MFQ axes) is non-trivial.
12918.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note WEIRD sample limitation; not a model-evaluation tool.

12853.note: Scherrer et al. 2023. 1767 moral scenarios (high+low ambiguity); MoralChoice (also appears in Fairness section). Inclusion under HC reflects 'moral belief evaluation' as human-centered.
critiques_received: MoReBench (2025): outcome-only choice; no procedural reasoning chain.
CMoralEval (2024): English; Western moral intuitions.
MFT-2 (2023): MFQ axes used externally without psychometric calibration.
12853.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag English; rule labels Western canonical; high-ambiguity items lack ground-truth.

12971.note: Huang et al. 2024. Tests LLM's emotional response across 8 negative emotions × 36 trigger scenarios using Positive and Negative Affect Schedule (PANAS) self-report instrument; 5 LLMs evaluated.
critiques_received: HeartBench (2025): emotional response ≠ empathic action; lacks anthropomorphic intelligence dimensions.
SMILE (2024): single-turn elicitation; no multi-turn supportive conversation.
Cross-cultural emotion psychology: PANAS is Western-validated; non-Western emotion lexicons absent.
12971.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note PANAS Western validation; LLM 'emotion' is anthropomorphic projection; multi-turn dynamics not tested.

10818.note: Chinese psychology examinations dataset for evaluating psychological knowledge in LLMs; 22k+ items from licensed Chinese psychology certification exams.
critiques_received: HeartBench (2025): exam knowledge ≠ empathic interaction; CPsyExam tests knowledge recall not crisis response.
SMILE (2024): single-turn QA; lacks supportive dialogue.
VITAL (2025): exam format doesn't capture clinical decision-making nuance.
10818.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note exam-knowledge limitation; doesn't test clinical empathy or crisis intervention.

12981.note: Qiu et al. SMILE: ChatGPT-expanded single-turn → multi-turn Chinese mental health support dialogues. ~ 20k multi-turn dialogues for evaluation/finetuning.
critiques_received: HeartBench (2025): focuses on supportive language not on professional boundary / referral.
VITAL (2025): not specifically aligned with clinical guidelines.
Educator-LLM (2025): role-specific (mental health); not generalizable.
12981.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note ChatGPT-generated dialogues may amplify model bias; Chinese only; no expert-validated clinical pathways.

12782.note: Probes 'core dimensions of anthropomorphic intelligence' in LLMs: cognitive empathy, affective empathy, sociopragmatic awareness etc. across multi-turn realistic scenarios.
critiques_received: MoReBench (2025): empathy benchmarks tend toward outcome scoring; HeartBench tries to address this but procedural eval still incomplete.
CPsyExam-style: HeartBench richer than knowledge tests but smaller scale.
12782.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag English; new benchmark, lacking longitudinal validation against human therapy outcomes.

10834.note: Yu et al. Chinese moral evaluation benchmark — 30K+ items grounded in Chinese cultural-moral fabric (儒家、仁义礼智信、地域族群伦理). Multi-perspective annotations.
critiques_received: NaVAB (2025): Chinese-only; cross-national value alignment unaddressed.
MoReBench (2025): outcome-style choice items dominate.
MFT critique: doesn't map to MFQ axes for cross-comparison.
10834.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note cultural specificity may limit generalization; some categories rely on subjective interpretation.

12862.note: Chiu et al. MoReBench: Evaluates **procedural** and **pluralistic** moral reasoning, not just outcomes. Tests whether models can articulate steps, consider multiple ethical frameworks, handle disagreement.
critiques_received: Recent: still English; small-scale; reliance on LLM-as-judge to score reasoning chains has variance.
12862.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note LLM-judge calibration; English; reasoning chain evaluation is labor-intensive.

12869.note: Raza et al. Human-centric framework for multimodal models. Evaluates models on alignment with human values, fairness, harm in image+text scenarios.
critiques_received: VITAL (2025): healthcare alignment more specialized.
TrustMH-Bench (2026): broader trustworthiness coverage needed.
12869.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note English; multimodal subset limited; LLM-as-judge dependency.

12951.note: Shetty et al. VITAL — 13K+ items for benchmarking pluralistic alignment in healthcare. Tests model's ability to honor multiple stakeholder values (patient/clinician/family/public) under conflict.
critiques_received: Recent benchmark; few public critiques. Healthcare specificity may limit cross-domain transfer.
12951.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note clinical specificity; English; pluralistic disagreement scoring is heuristic.

12964.note: Jiang et al. (EMNLP 2025): Educator-role moral and normative LLM profiling. Tests whether LLMs uphold profession-specific ethical norms (e.g., teacher confidentiality, child protection).
critiques_received: TrustMH-Bench (2026): single-role benchmark; multi-stakeholder needed.
VITAL (2025): healthcare profession parallel; cross-profession comparison absent.
12964.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note role-specific limits; no longitudinal validation.

13481.note: Shi et al. Multi-national value alignment: 5-country (US/UK/CN/FR/DE) news + value statements; tests whether LLMs align with national mainstream values across abortion legalization, immigration, etc.
critiques_received: CMoralEval (2024): mainstream-value framing may suppress minority moral voices.
MoReBench (2025): national-value alignment is outcome-focused; procedural pluralism untested.
MFQ-2 (2023): MFQ axes provide better psychometric scaffold than news-derived statements.
13481.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note 5-country sample; mainstream-stance label suppresses pluralism; news source biases.

13502.note: Xiong et al. Comprehensive trustworthiness benchmark for LLMs in mental-health contexts. Combines safety (crisis), professionalism (boundary), empathy (cognitive+affective), pluralism, refusal/over-refusal.
critiques_received: Recent (preprint); few critiques yet.
HeartBench (2025) / SMILE (2024) precursors: TrustMH-Bench broader but less deep on each axis.
13502.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note Chinese+English; LLM-as-judge dependency; mental-health professional validation incomplete.
